page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.s3 server family. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 4 physical cores, with 1.0 thread per core, indicating that hyperthreading is disabled. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.s3.large is structured for compute-focused tasks that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of hyperthreading. The absence of bundled local storage means that persistent storage must be attached externally. From a resource density perspective, the dedicated physical core mapping provides predictable CPU performance, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require stable compute resources without the memory footprint of general-purpose instances. Suitable workloads include lightweight compute tasks, web serving, batch processing, and basic application hosting.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.s3 family, operating on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external storage attachments for persistent data. This hardware profile is designed for compute-focused workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores but do not demand large memory capacities. The ecs.s3.large is suited for lightweight web serving, batch processing, and dedicated application hosting where predictable CPU performance is required.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.large is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 8.0 GB of RAM on a KVM hypervisor, designed for compute-focused workloads without local storage.

tagline: A dedicated four-core x86_64 compute instance with a one-to-two processor-to-memory ratio and no bundled local storage.
